description: Screencap v1 — Lightweight Screen Memory for macOS. Full implementation from SPEC.md.

userStories.title: Cargo workspace skeleton and config parsing
userStories.description: Set up the Rust project with Cargo.toml, dependencies, module structure, and TOML config parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: Cargo.toml with dependencies: tokio, axum, clap, rusqlite, serde, serde_json, toml, anyhow, thiserror, chrono, uuid, reqwest, rust-embed, tracing, tracing-subscriber
src/main.rs with clap CLI skeleton (subcommands: start, stop, status, now, today, search, mcp, config, costs, prune, export)
src/config.rs parses ~/.screencap/config.toml matching SPEC.md config format with sensible defaults
Creates ~/.screencap/ directory structure on first run if it doesn't exist
Module stubs exist for capture/, pipeline/, ai/, storage/, api/, mcp/, export/
Unit tests for config: loading defaults when no config file exists, parsing a valid config.toml, error on invalid TOML syntax
cargo check passes
cargo clippy passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundation. Every other story depends on this.

userStories.title: SQLite schema, migrations, and typed models
userStories.description: Implement the storage layer with all tables from SPEC.md, FTS5 indexes, and Rust model structs.
acceptanceCriteria: src/storage/db.rs creates screencap.db with all tables: captures, extractions, extraction_batches, insights
FTS5 virtual tables: search_index and insights_fts
All indexes from SPEC.md schema created
src/storage/models.rs has typed Rust structs for Capture, Extraction, ExtractionBatch, Insight
CRUD functions: insert_capture, get_captures_by_timerange, get_pending_captures, update_capture_status
CRUD functions: insert_extraction, insert_extraction_batch, insert_insight
FTS search function: search_extractions(query) returning ranked results
Unit tests for insert and query operations using in-memory SQLite
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: Reference SPEC.md Storage Layer section for exact schema.

userStories.title: Swift bridge for ScreenCaptureKit screenshots
userStories.description: Create the Swift bridge that captures screenshots from all displays using ScreenCaptureKit.
acceptanceCriteria: swift/Sources/ScreenCapture.swift wraps SCShareableContent and SCScreenshotManager
C-callable function: capture_screenshot(display_id, output_path, quality) -> bool
C-callable function: get_display_count() -> i32
swift/Sources/bridge.h with C header declarations
build.rs or Makefile compiles Swift to a static library linkable from Rust
src/capture/screenshot.rs calls the Swift functions via FFI
Saves JPEG to specified path at configurable quality
Compile-time feature flag: cfg(feature = 'mock-capture') that replaces the Swift FFI call with a stub that generates a solid-color JPEG (for testing without Screen Recording permission)
cargo check passes (Swift library links correctly)
cargo test passes (using mock-capture feature)
userStories.notes: ScreenCaptureKit requires Screen Recording permission. The agent likely won't have TCC approval, so the mock-capture feature flag is essential for testability. The fallback generates a synthetic JPEG so downstream pipeline code (extraction, API screenshot serving) works identically.

userStories.title: Swift bridge for active window metadata
userStories.description: Create the Swift bridge that retrieves the current frontmost app name, window title, and bundle ID.
acceptanceCriteria: swift/Sources/WindowInfo.swift uses NSWorkspace to get frontmost application info
C-callable function: get_active_window(out_app_name, out_window_title, out_bundle_id) -> bool
src/capture/window.rs calls the Swift function and returns a WindowInfo struct
WindowInfo struct has: app_name: String, window_title: String, bundle_id: String
Handles cases where window title is unavailable gracefully
Same mock-capture feature flag: cfg(feature = 'mock-capture') returns a fake WindowInfo (app_name='MockApp', window_title='Mock Window', bundle_id='com.mock.app')
cargo check passes
cargo test passes (using mock-capture feature)
userStories.notes: NSWorkspace.shared.frontmostApplication for the app, CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo for the window title. The mock feature flag is shared with US-003 — same flag gates both screenshot and window info mocks.

userStories.title: Timer-based capture loop with persistence
userStories.description: Implement the core capture daemon loop: take a screenshot on a timer, grab window metadata, save JPEG, insert capture row.
acceptanceCriteria: src/daemon.rs runs a tokio loop that captures on the configured idle_interval_secs
Each capture: takes screenshot, gets window metadata, saves JPEG to ~/.screencap/screenshots/YYYY/MM/DD/HHMMSS-{display_id}.jpg
Inserts capture row into SQLite with extraction_status='pending'
Respects excluded_apps and excluded_window_titles from config (skips capture if frontmost app is excluded)
Dedup: skips capture if app_name + window_title haven't changed since last capture AND less than idle_interval elapsed
Creates date-partitioned screenshot directories automatically
Logs captures via tracing
Unit test: excluded app is skipped
Unit test: dedup skips unchanged context
Unit test: screenshot path follows YYYY/MM/DD/HHMMSS-{display_id}.jpg convention
cargo check passes
cargo clippy passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: Start with timer-only. Event-driven capture (CGEventTap) is a later enhancement. Use a mock/stub for the Swift screenshot function in tests.

userStories.title: Daemon lifecycle: start, stop, status CLI commands
userStories.description: Implement daemon process management so screencap can run in the background.
acceptanceCriteria: screencap (no subcommand) starts the daemon in the foreground with capture loop + API server
screencap start forks to background using fork()/setsid() (or nohup-style) and writes PID to ~/.screencap/screencap.pid
screencap stop sends shutdown signal to running daemon
screencap status shows: running/stopped, PID, uptime, capture count today, storage usage
PID file written to ~/.screencap/screencap.pid for process management
Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT
cargo check passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: REST API: health, stats, and capture endpoints
userStories.description: Stand up the axum HTTP server with basic read endpoints for captures and system info.
acceptanceCriteria: Axum server starts on localhost:7878 as part of the daemon
GET /api/health returns 200 with {status: 'ok', uptime_secs: N}
GET /api/stats returns capture count, storage bytes, uptime, captures today
GET /api/captures?from=&to=&app=&limit=&offset= returns paginated captures
GET /api/captures/:id returns single capture with metadata
GET /api/screenshots/* serves JPEG files from the screenshots directory
GET /api/apps returns list of app names with capture counts
All endpoints return JSON with proper error handling
Integration test: start server, insert test capture via db, GET /api/captures returns it, GET /api/captures/:id returns it, GET /api/health returns 200
cargo check passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: Use tokio::test with a test helper that spins up the axum server on a random port.

userStories.title: LlmProvider trait and OpenAI-compatible client
userStories.description: Implement the AI provider abstraction and the openai_compat client that covers OpenRouter, OpenAI, and LM Studio.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ai/provider.rs defines LlmProvider trait with: complete(prompt, images) -> Result<String> and complete_text(prompt) -> Result<String>
src/ai/openai_compat.rs implements LlmProvider for OpenAI-compatible APIs
Supports sending images as base64 in the chat completions format
base_url configurable: defaults to OpenRouter (openrouter.ai/api/v1), OpenAI (api.openai.com/v1), LM Studio (localhost:1234/v1)
Reads API key from environment variable specified in config
Returns token usage counts from the API response
Error handling for rate limits, auth failures, network errors
Provider factory function: create_provider(config) -> Box<dyn LlmProvider>
Unit test: create_provider with provider='openrouter' returns an instance that compiles and has correct base_url
Unit test: create_provider with provider='lmstudio' returns an instance with base_url localhost:1234
Unit test: MockLlmProvider implementation in src/ai/mock.rs (or tests/) that returns configurable canned responses — reusable by all pipeline tests
Integration test: MockLlmProvider.complete() returns expected string, MockLlmProvider.complete_text() returns expected string
cargo check passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: OpenRouter uses the same API format as OpenAI. The only difference is the base_url and model ID format (provider/model).

userStories.title: Extraction pipeline: prompt construction and response parsing
userStories.description: Build the extraction prompt template and the typed JSON response parser, testable without a live LLM.
acceptanceCriteria: src/pipeline/prompts.rs contains the extraction prompt template from SPEC.md
src/pipeline/extraction.rs has build_extraction_prompt(captures, window_metadata) -> String
src/pipeline/extraction.rs has parse_extraction_response(json_str) -> Result<ExtractionResult>

ExtractionResult struct matches SPEC.md: frames vec (capture_id, activity_type, description, app_context, project, topics, people, key_content, sentiment) + batch_summary (primary_activity, project_context, narrative)
Unit test: build_extraction_prompt with 3 mock captures produces valid prompt containing capture metadata
Unit test: parse_extraction_response with a golden JSON fixture parses into correct typed struct
Unit test: parse_extraction_response with malformed JSON returns descriptive error, does not panic
cargo check passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: This must be testable with canned fixtures. No LLM calls in this story.

userStories.title: Extraction pipeline: scheduler, persistence, and FTS updates
userStories.description: Wire up the extraction scheduler that batches pending captures, calls the LLM, persists results, and updates FTS.
acceptanceCriteria: src/pipeline/scheduler.rs runs extraction on the configured interval_secs
Queries pending captures from SQLite, batches up to max_images_per_batch
Reads screenshot JPEGs from disk and encodes as base64 for the LLM
Sends batch to vision LLM via LlmProvider.complete(prompt, images)
On success: persists extraction rows per frame, extraction_batch row with narrative, cost, model_used, tokens_used
On success: updates capture rows to extraction_status='processed' and links extraction_id
On success: inserts into FTS search_index (description, key_content, narrative, project, topics)
On LLM failure: marks captures as extraction_status='failed', logs error, continues to next batch
On parse failure: marks captures as 'failed', stores raw_response in extraction_batches for debugging

Integration test: insert 5 pending captures, run extraction with a MockLlmProvider that returns golden JSON, verify all 5 captures are 'processed', verify extractions and extraction_batch rows exist, verify FTS search finds content
cargo check passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: Create a MockLlmProvider in tests that returns canned responses. This mock will be reused by synthesis tests.

userStories.title: Synthesis pipeline: rolling context generation
userStories.description: Implement the rolling context synthesis that runs every 30 minutes and answers 'what am I doing right now?'
acceptanceCriteria: src/pipeline/synthesis.rs generates rolling context from the last 30 min of extractions
src/pipeline/prompts.rs contains the rolling context prompt template
Sends extraction batch summaries + frame data to the synthesis LLM (text-only, no images via LlmProvider.complete_text)
Parses response into the rolling context JSON format from SPEC.md (current_focus, active_project, apps_used, context_switches, mood, summary)
Persists as an insight row with type='rolling', window_start, window_end, and narrative extracted for FTS
Scheduler triggers this on the configured rolling_interval_secs
Tracks token usage and cost
Unit test: parse rolling context golden JSON into typed struct
Integration test: insert mock extractions, run rolling context with MockLlmProvider, verify insight row with type='rolling' exists in DB
cargo check passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Synthesis pipeline: hourly digest generation
userStories.description: Implement hourly digest synthesis that summarizes each hour of activity.
acceptanceCriteria: Synthesis module generates hourly digest from the last hour of extraction batches
Prompt template produces the hourly JSON format from SPEC.md (projects, topics, people, key_moments, focus_score, narrative)
Persists as an insight row with type='hourly'
Runs at the top of each hour (or close to it)
Skips hours with no extractions (does not create empty insight rows)
Tracks token usage and cost
Integration test: insert mock extraction batches spanning one hour, run hourly digest with MockLlmProvider, verify insight row with type='hourly' exists with correct window_start/window_end
cargo check passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Synthesis pipeline: daily summary generation

acceptanceCriteria: Synthesis module generates daily summary from all hourly digests for the day

Persists as an insight row with type='daily'
Triggers at the configured daily_summary_time
Also triggerable on-demand via CLI (screencap today)

cargo test passes

userStories.title: REST API: insights and search endpoints
userStories.description: Add the insights and search endpoints to the API server.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/insights/current returns the latest rolling context insight
GET /api/insights/hourly?date= returns all hourly digests for a day
GET /api/insights/daily?date= returns the daily summary
GET /api/insights/daily?from=&to= returns a range of daily summaries
GET /api/insights/projects?from=&to= returns project time allocation aggregated from extractions
GET /api/insights/topics?from=&to= returns topic frequency from extractions
GET /api/search?q=&app=&project=&from=&to=&limit= does FTS search across extractions

cargo test passes

userStories.title: CLI read commands: now, today, yesterday, week, search, projects, costs
userStories.description: Implement the CLI commands that read from the database and display results.
acceptanceCriteria: screencap now prints the latest rolling context summary to stdout
screencap today prints today's daily summary (generates on-demand if not yet created)
screencap yesterday prints yesterday's daily summary
screencap week prints this week's daily summaries
screencap search 'query' --project X --app Y --last 24h does FTS search and prints results
screencap projects [--last 7d] shows project time allocation
screencap costs shows AI API cost breakdown (total, by day, by pipeline stage)
Output is formatted nicely for terminal (colors via tracing or similar)
Unit test: 'screencap now' against a DB with a rolling insight returns the expected narrative text to stdout
Unit test: 'screencap search' against a DB with seeded extractions returns matching results
Unit test: 'screencap costs' against a DB with extraction_batches rows shows total cost

userStories.title: Svelte web UI: project setup and app shell
userStories.description: Set up the Svelte project with routing, the app shell layout, and embed it in the Rust binary.
acceptanceCriteria: web/ directory with Svelte project (vite + svelte)
App shell with navigation: Timeline, Insights, Search, Stats tabs
Client-side routing between the four views (svelte-spa-router or similar)
Shared API client module that talks to localhost:7878/api/*
npm run build produces static files in web/dist/
Rust binary embeds web/dist/ via rust-embed and serves at GET /
Fallback to index.html for SPA routing (GET /timeline, /insights, /search, /stats all serve index.html)
build.rs or Makefile documents the build order: npm run build (web/) THEN cargo build
npm run build succeeds with zero errors
npm run check (svelte-check) passes with zero errors
cargo check passes (with embedded assets)
Integration test: start server, GET / returns HTML containing the Svelte app shell, GET /api/health still returns JSON

Browser verification via expect MCP: open localhost:7878, verify app shell renders with navigation tabs (Timeline, Insights, Search, Stats), click each tab and verify the URL changes and corresponding view container appears, verify no console errors, run accessibility_audit with 0 critical/serious violations, close
userStories.notes: Keep the UI clean and minimal. No heavy component libraries. The build order matters: Svelte must compile before cargo build embeds the dist/ files. Use expect MCP tools for browser verification. IMPORTANT: Read and follow the frontend-design skill at .agents/skills/frontend-design/SKILL.md before writing any Svelte/CSS code. Design must be distinctive and production-grade, not generic AI aesthetics.

Use expect MCP tools (open, playwright, screenshot, console_logs, accessibility_audit, close) — not raw browser tools. IMPORTANT: Read and follow the frontend-design skill at .agents/skills/frontend-design/SKILL.md before writing any Svelte/CSS code. userStories.title: MCP server with stdio transport

Tool: get_current_context returns latest rolling context

Tool: get_screenshot with param (id) returns base64-encoded JPEG
Tool: get_daily_summary with param (date)
Tool: get_project_activity with params (project, from, to)
Tool: get_app_usage with params (from, to)
Proper JSON-RPC message handling over stdio
Registrable via: claude mcp add screencap -- screencap mcp

userStories.title: Markdown export and daily log files

screencap export --date YYYY-MM-DD writes markdown to stdout or --output path
screencap export --last 7d exports a range

userStories.title: Pruning, retention, and storage management
userStories.description: Implement auto-pruning of old captures and the prune CLI command.
acceptanceCriteria: screencap prune --older-than 90d deletes captures, extractions, insights, and screenshot files older than the threshold
Auto-prune runs on daemon startup if max_age_days is configured
Deletes both SQLite rows and corresponding JPEG files
Logs what was deleted and bytes reclaimed
Does NOT delete insights of type='daily' (they're cheap to keep as historical record)
Integration test: insert 5 captures with screenshot JPEGs on disk + extraction rows, prune with threshold that matches all 5, verify DB rows are deleted AND JPEG files no longer exist on disk
Integration test: insert a daily insight older than threshold, verify prune does NOT delete it
cargo check passes
cargo test passes
userStories.notes: The JPEG deletion test is critical — it's easy to forget to delete filesystem artifacts when pruning DB rows.

userStories.title: End-to-end pipeline integration test
userStories.description: A single integration test that exercises the complete pipeline: capture → extraction → synthesis → API → search → MCP, all wired together.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/e2e_pipeline.rs exists with a #[tokio::test] that uses a temp dir and in-memory or temp-file SQLite
Step 1: Insert 10 mock capture rows with synthetic screenshot JPEGs on disk, each with different app_name/window_title combos
Step 2: Run extraction pipeline with MockLlmProvider (returns canned extraction JSON) — verify all 10 captures become 'processed', verify extraction rows exist, verify FTS index has entries
Step 3: Run rolling context synthesis with MockLlmProvider — verify insight with type='rolling' exists
Step 4: Run hourly digest synthesis — verify insight with type='hourly' exists
Step 5: Run daily summary synthesis — verify insight with type='daily' exists

Step 6: Start the axum API server on a random port against the same DB, make real HTTP requests: GET /api/health (200), GET /api/captures (returns 10), GET /api/search?q=<keyword from mock extraction> (returns >0 results), GET /api/insights/current (returns rolling context), GET /api/insights/daily?date=<today> (returns daily summary)
Step 7: Verify that GET / returns HTML (embedded Svelte app), GET /api/screenshots/<path> returns a JPEG
The entire test runs in <5 seconds (no real LLM calls, no real screenshots)
cargo test --test e2e_pipeline passes
userStories.notes: This is the most important story. If this passes, the core loop works. Use the same MockLlmProvider from US-009b tests.

userStories.title: Daemon startup smoke test
userStories.description: Verify that the daemon binary starts up, begins all schedulers, serves the API, and shuts down cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/e2e_daemon.rs (or a shell script invoked by cargo test) starts the screencap binary in foreground mode with a test config pointing to a temp dir
Verifies: API server responds on configured port within 3 seconds of startup
Verifies: GET /api/health returns 200
Verifies: GET /api/stats returns valid JSON with capture_count field
Verifies: GET / returns HTML (Svelte app served)
Sends SIGTERM and verifies process exits cleanly within 5 seconds
Verifies: PID file is cleaned up on shutdown
Verifies: no panics in stderr output
cargo test --test e2e_daemon passes
userStories.notes: This test must use a unique port and temp data directory to avoid conflicts. Use the screencap binary built by cargo. Skip actual screen capture (mock the Swift bridge or use a feature flag).

userStories.title: Edge case and error resilience tests
userStories.description: Test error paths that individual stories may have missed: empty DB, missing config, failed LLM, corrupt images, concurrent access.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: extraction scheduler with 0 pending captures does nothing (no error, no LLM call)
Test: synthesis with 0 extractions does nothing (no error, no LLM call)
Test: LLM provider returns HTTP 429 (rate limit) — extraction retries or marks as failed, does not crash
Test: LLM provider returns HTTP 401 (bad API key) — logged as error, captures stay as 'pending', does not crash
Test: LLM provider returns garbage (non-JSON) — captures marked 'failed', raw response stored, does not crash
Test: screenshot file referenced in capture row is missing from disk — extraction skips it gracefully
Test: config file does not exist — defaults are used, daemon starts successfully
Test: config file exists but is empty — defaults are used, daemon starts successfully
Test: pruning with 0 matching captures does nothing (no error)
Test: CLI commands (now, today, search) against an empty database return helpful messages, not errors
All cargo test passes
userStories.notes: Many of these might already be covered by earlier story tests. This story is specifically to audit and fill gaps.

userStories.title: Full browser QA: cross-view, responsive, performance
userStories.description: End-to-end browser verification of all UI views using expect MCP tools. Tests the full user journey across all views with seeded data, responsive viewports, and performance/accessibility audits.
acceptanceCriteria: Start the daemon with seeded data: at least 5 captures with screenshots, 5 extractions, 3 extraction_batches with cost data, 1 rolling insight, 3 hourly insights, 1 daily insight
Browser test flow via expect MCP (open localhost:7878):
1. App shell: verify navigation renders, all 4 tabs clickable
2. Timeline: verify captures grouped by hour, thumbnails visible, click to expand works, filter by app works
3. Insights: verify rolling context card, hourly cards expandable, daily summary section visible
4. Search: type a query, verify results appear, verify no-results state for gibberish query
5. Stats: verify charts render (canvas elements non-empty), cost section shows data
6. Cross-view navigation: navigate Timeline → Search → Insights → Stats → Timeline without errors
Responsive check: test at 375x812 (mobile) and 1280x800 (laptop) viewports — no horizontal overflow, navigation still accessible, text readable
Performance check via performance_metrics: LCP under 2.5s, CLS under 0.1
Final console_logs check: 0 console errors across entire session
Final accessibility_audit: 0 critical or serious WCAG violations
close the browser session
userStories.notes: This is the UI equivalent of US-030 (pipeline e2e). If this passes, the UI works end-to-end. Must use expect MCP tools exclusively. Delegate to a subagent if available. The frontend-design skill at .agents/skills/frontend-design/SKILL.md defines the design quality bar the UI must meet.

userStories.title: Full build and lint green check
userStories.description: Final verification that the entire project compiles, passes all tests, and has no lint warnings.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo check passes with zero warnings
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes (all warnings are errors)
cargo test passes (all unit + integration + e2e tests)
cargo build --release succeeds
cd web && npm run check passes with zero errors
cd web && npm run build produces web/dist/ with index.html
No TODO or FIXME comments left in src/ that indicate unfinished work (grep -r 'TODO\|FIXME' src/ returns 0 results, or all are deliberate future enhancements clearly marked)
README.md exists with: project description, build instructions, configuration guide, CLI usage examples
userStories.notes: This is the final gate. If this passes, the project is shippable.
